video:video.video:title: Prince's first wife Mayte on keeping his legacy alive a decade after his death | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ten years after Prince's death, his first wife Mayte Jannell Garcia has relaunched the Live 4 Love charity that she and the pop star first established after the 1996 death of their infant child.

video:video.video:title: Researchers create new potato varieties to strengthen nation’s chip supply
video:video.video:description: Researchers spend decades developing potatoes for chip makers that can grow in all kinds of climates, avoid diseases and pests, sit in storage for months and still deliver a satisfying crunch (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Iranians in Turkey trapped in limbo as war raises the stakes of an uncertain existence
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of Iranians live in neighboring Turkey, drawn by economic opportunities and relative stability.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 22
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, April 22nd: Iran fires at container ship in Strait of Hormuz; Virginia passes House redistricting plan; Southern Poverty Law Center accused of funneling money to hate group leaders; Hegseth says military personnel won’t have to get flu vaccine.

video:video.video:title: Prince's first wife keeps his legacy alive with music charity
video:video.video:description: On the 10th anniversary of his death, Prince's first wife Mayte Jannell Garcia explains why she is relaunching their Live 4 Love Charities.

video:video.video:title: Georgia wildfires destroy dozens of homes, force evacuations
video:video.video:description: Brianna Elliot, who lives in Brantley County, Georgia, fears her home and her pets are gone after a rapidly-spreading wildfire engulfed the areas surrounding her home.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo offers message of hope to inmates on Equatorial Guinea prison visit
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV told inmates at one of Equatorial Guinea’s notorious prisons that they are not alone, as he drew attention Wednesday to prison conditions, human rights abuses and injustices that campaigners have denounced for years here.

video:video.video:title: Wildfires burn in Florida
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. were intensifying today. Some of the biggest blazes are along Georgia’s coast and around Jacksonville, Florida, a state that is facing one of its worst fire seasons in decades.

video:video.video:title: Large wildfire burns in Clinch County, Georgia, prompting evacuations
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. forced more people to flee Wednesday after destroying nearly 50 homes in Georgia and causing school closings.

video:video.video:title: Brazil’s 80-year-old Lula hits the treadmill to ease voter concerns about age
video:video.video:description: Brazil’s President Lula da Silva is boosting his reelection bid at age 80 by showcasing his fitness. Critics say his workouts are more popular than he is.

video:video.video:title: Streep, Hathaway dazzle at London 'Prada' premiere
video:video.video:description: The main cast of "The Devil Wears Prada" showed off their style at the film's London premiere. (April 22)

video:video.video:title: Runner collapses near Boston Marathon finish line and two competitors help him finish the race
video:video.video:description: Ajay Haridasse was close to finishing the Boston Marathon when his legs gave out. But as he struggled to get up, two other runners came along, lifted him up and helped him across the finish line.

video:video.video:title: CEO of Boston Athletic Association says 'camaraderie in running' evident at marathon
video:video.video:description: When a Boston Marathon runner's legs gave out, two other runners helped him cross the finish line. Jack Fleming, CEO of the Boston Athletic Association, says that type of kindness and camaraderie is typical among marathon runners.

video:video.video:title: More than 1,100 dancers gather for Radio City Rockettes auditions in New York
video:video.video:description: More than 1,100 dancers showed off their eye-high kicks and pirouettes as they auditioned for a spot with the iconic Radio City Rockettes.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 22nd: Iran fires on 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz; Pope Leo XIV visits Equatorial Guinea; Warner shareholders set to vote on Paramount's $81 billion takeover; Sony's AI robot is beating human pros at table tennis.

video:video.video:title: Sweden's annual secondhand fashion swaps help to cut environmental waste
video:video.video:description: Sweden is trying to tackle garment waste by encouraging people to go for secondhand fashion. Clothes swapping events are growing in popularity as people reduce the environmental impact of their wardrobes.

video:video.video:title: Barry Levinson looks back on 'Sleepers'
video:video.video:description: Director Barry Levinson says "it's shocking" that it’s been 30 years since he made “Sleepers,” the 1996 drama starring Brad Pitt, Robert De Niro, Dustin Hoffman and Billy Crudup.

video:video.video:title: 'Beauty will save the world': 30th Moscow Art fair opens with wide range of work on display
video:video.video:description: The 30th annual Art Moscow fair opened on Wednesday in Russia's capital. After starting out as a strictly modern art event in 1996, today among the fair's exhibits, were a wide specter of both classical paintings and contemporary art objects.

video:video.video:title: Doctors are using AI to improve and personalize ovarian cancer treatment
video:video.video:description: A global team of doctors and researchers are using AI to better improve the treatment and survival rates of women with ovarian cancer.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire burns in South Florida as the state faces one of its worst fire seasons in decades
video:video.video:description: Firefighters in Florida are battling 131 wildfires that have burned 34 square miles. A long drought, low humidity, and strong winds are fueling the fires.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and snowmelt are hurtling large chunks of ice into Michigan homes
video:video.video:description: Large chunks of ice rammed into homes along Michigan’s Black Lake in the northeastern Lower Peninsula as constant spring rainfall and winter melt left many homes flooded and threatened to overflow stressed dam systems.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo given colorful welcome before leading Mass in Mongomo in eastern Equatorial Guinea
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV held Mass in the eastern Equatorial Guinea city of Mongomo on Wednesday as he continued his visit to the country and his African tour.

video:video.video:title: Closely divided Congress reacts to Virginia redistricting result that could help Democrats
video:video.video:description: Republicans and Democrats in a closely divided Congress are reacting to Virginia voters approving a mid-decade redistricting plan that could boost Democrats’ chances of winning four additional U.S.

video:video.video:title: Virginia voters approve redistricting plan that could boost Democrats’ seats in Congress
video:video.video:description: Virginia voters approved a mid-decade redistricting plan on Tuesday that could boost Democrats’ chances of winning four additional U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP photographer reflects on the legacy of Chernobyl after 4 decades of coverage
video:video.video:description: Efrem Lukatsky, a photographer for The Associated Press, has chronicled the 1986 Chernobyl disaster from its earliest days, making many trips to the “exclusion zone” around the damaged nuclear power plant.

video:video.video:title: Khartoum remains quiet three years into the war in Sudan
video:video.video:description: Three years into the war and a year after Khartoum was taken over by the Sudanese army, the battered heart of the capital city remains quiet.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan maintaining optimism US-Iran talks can go ahead after ceasefire extension
video:video.video:description: Pakistan had planned to host a second round of talks, but the White House suspended Vice President JD Vance’s planned trip to Islamabad as Iran rebuffed efforts to restart negotiations.

video:video.video:title: Here's how to save money on flights as airlines raise prices amid the Iran war
video:video.video:description: For budget-conscious travelers, it’s gotten harder to escape the rising cost of airfare due to the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Lipstick and pajamas are among over 6,000 products that could cost more due to war
video:video.video:description: Did you know the war in Iran could raise the prices of some of your favourite everyday items? Spiking oil prices have been the biggest impact on those outside the conflict zone so far

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and snowmelt is pushing large chunks of ice into northeastern Michigan homes
video:video.video:description: Homes have flooded along Black Lake in Michigan's northeastern Lower Peninsula as spring rains and winter melt overflow stressed dam systems

video:video.video:title: Uncertainty prevails in Tehran after Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely
video:video.video:description: Uncertainty over the future of the standoff with the U.S. prevailed in Tehran on Wednesday, hours after a decision by the U.S president to extend a ceasefire while keeping a naval blockade in place.

video:video.video:title: Chinese artist marks World Earth Day with annual performance art presentation
video:video.video:description: Wearing dinosaur-shaped balloons Chinese artist Kong Ning marks Earth Day in Beijing on Wednesday with her annual art installation performance.

video:video.video:title: Smallest deer, biggest aww: Pudu born at San Diego Zoo Safari Park
video:video.video:description: A newborn pudu fawn -- one of the world’s tiniest deer species -- has arrived at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park.

video:video.video:title: A robot is beating human pros at table tennis. Its maker calls it a milestone for machines
video:video.video:description: A paddle-wielding robot is so adept at playing table tennis that it is posing a tough challenge to elite human players and sometimes defeating them, according to a new study that shows how advances in artificial intelligence are making robots more agile.

video:video.video:title: Heated Rivalry' author and director on its crazy success and series future
video:video.video:description: The "Heated Rivalry" author and director-screenwriter were sensations at BookCon in New York. (April 22)

video:video.video:title: Senate Banking Committee quizzes Fed nominee Warsh over economy, independence
video:video.video:description: Senators on Tuesday quizzed President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve about whether he is able to stay independent of the president.

video:video.video:title: Medicine for sick people in Sudan is stuck because of the Iran war's effect on supply lines
video:video.video:description: The latest war in the Middle East has far-reaching effects, including on countries ravaged by conflicts of their own.

video:video.video:title: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving in latest departure of a top defense leader
video:video.video:description: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his job, the Pentagon abruptly announced Wednesday, the first head of a military service to depart during President Donald Trump’s second term but just the latest top defense leader to step down or be ousted.

video:video.video:title: Charlize Theron: risk tolerant parent
video:video.video:description: "Apex" star Charlize Theron, who did her own rock climbing in the action thriller, explains her approach to her daughters' outdoor activities.

video:video.video:title: Chinese auto battery maker CATL unveils latest tech as EV exports soar
video:video.video:description: China's battery giant CATL rolled out its latest innovations and strategic vision ahead of an annual international auto show.

video:video.video:title: Groundbreaking begins on new LA2028 Olympics cricket stadium
video:video.video:description: Construction has begun on a new 10,000-seat stadium in Pomona, California, which will serve as the venue for men’s and women’s cricket during the Olympics in 2028.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 23
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, April 23: Iran fires on at least three boats in Strait of Hormuz; Israel and Lebanon to meet in Washington; Train collision in Denmark; Candidates for governor debate in California.

video:video.video:title: Streep, Hathaway dazzle at London 'Prada' premiere
video:video.video:description: The stars of "The Devil Wears Prada 2" showed off their style at the sequel's European premiere in London.

video:video.video:title: Two killed and one in serious condition after chemical leak at a West Virginia plant, officials say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a chemical leak at a West Virginia plant has killed two people and sent about 30 others to the hospital, including one person in serious condition.

video:video.video:title: Charlize Theron celebrates daughters’ differences, inspires fearless living
video:video.video:description: Charlize Theron walked the red carpet for her new Netflix film "Apex" Wednesday in New York. The actor, who did her own rock climbing in the action thriller, talked about the differences between her two daughters.

video:video.video:title: Views across southern Lebanon show widespread destruction from Israel's razing of villages
video:video.video:description: Footage on Wednesday showed widespread destruction in parts of southern Lebanon following Israel's invasion of the area to carry out military operations against Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Fashionistas rejoice: Devil Wears Prada returns to Milan's big screens
video:video.video:description: As fashionistas gear up for the return of the Devil Wears Prada to cinemas, the buzz has hit Milan. The Italian city is the birthplace of the luxury designer brand immortalized in the movie's title.

video:video.video:title: Shipping ramifications of Iran war compound suffering for the sick in war-torn Sudan
video:video.video:description: In a small clinic in a remote village in rural Sudan, the shelves of the pharmacy were starting to empty out, with the clinic unsure about when the next shipment of medicines would arrive.

video:video.video:title: Celeste Rivas Hernandez, girl who singer D4vd is charged with killing, died from penetrating wounds
video:video.video:description: Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the 14-year-old girl, singer D4vd is charged with killing, died from penetrating injuries, according to an autopsy report released Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Shipping ramifications of Iran war compound suffering for the sick in war-torn Sudan
video:video.video:description: In a small clinic in a remote village in rural Sudan, the shelves of the pharmacy were starting to empty out, with the clinic unsure about when the next shipment of medicines would arrive.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans recycling group turns glass bottles into sand in fight against land loss
video:video.video:description: A New Orleans company has created a system that turns glass bottles into sand to help fight Louisiana's land loss crisis. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: Iran attacks ship in Strait of Hormuz after Trump indefinitely extends the ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, damaging the vessel and complicating diplomatic efforts to bring the U.S. and Iran to Pakistan for talks.

video:video.video:title: In climate change fight, doomerism is out. Laughter is in
video:video.video:description: Across the world, groups of activists, teachers and psychologists are tackling one of the world’s most daunting problems — human-caused climate change — with laughter, dancing, hugs and most especially joy.

video:video.video:title: Warsh says he got no pressure from Trump to cut rates even as president publicly pushes for them
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve Kevin Warsh said Tuesday that he never promised the White House that he would cut interest rates, even as the president renewed his calls for the central bank to do so.

video:video.video:title: Climate doom and gloom? Try laughing instead. Activists embrace joy in the fight to save Earth
video:video.video:description: Across the world, groups of activists, teachers and psychologists are tackling one of the world’s most daunting problems — human-caused climate change — with laughter, dancing, hugs and most especially joy.

video:video.video:title: Iranian State TV airs what it says is video of Iran’s Navy seizing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iranian State TV on Thursday aired what it said was video of Iran’s Navy seizing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians resort to burning cooking oil, plastic, to prepare meals and stay warm
video:video.video:description: Many shelter in makeshift tents and damaged buildings, using homemade stoves fueled by whatever scraps of wood, plastic and other materials that can be scavenged.

video:video.video:title: Captain of ship stranded in Strait of Hormuz for over 50 days describes ordeal of crew
video:video.video:description: A captain on board one of hundreds of ships stranded in the Persian Gulf on Wednesday said his crew has been under "constant stress" since the war started.

video:video.video:title: 2 trains collide in Denmark, prompting a massive emergency response north of Copenhagen
video:video.video:description: Two trains collided in Denmark early Thursday, prompting a massive emergency response for what police called a major accident.

video:video.video:title: China is rapidly embracing AI across many tech sectors
video:video.video:description: From police patrol robots to everyday consumer gadgets, artificial intelligence is taking centre stage at this year’s Canton Fair in China.

video:video.video:title: Samsung workers rally in South Korea, demanding higher bonuses and threatening strike
video:video.video:description: Thousands of unionized workers from Samsung Electronics rallied Thursday at its semiconductor complex in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, demanding higher bonuses and threatening to strike as the booming demand for artificial intelligence drives up memory-chip profits.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry arrives in Ukraine capital on unannounced visit
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry arrived in Ukraine on Thursday. Local media reported he is on an unannounced visit to take part in the Kyiv Security Forum.

video:video.video:title: California's woes at the center of debate among leading candidates for governor
video:video.video:description: Six leading candidates for California governor are jostling for advantage in a chaotic race with no clear leader.

video:video.video:title: US military says it seizes another oil tanker associated with Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military on Thursday seized another tanker associated with the smuggling of Iranian oil, ratcheting up a standoff with Iran a day after its paramilitary Revolutionary Guards took control of two vessels in the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Senate passes budget plan for ICE and Border Patrol in bid to reopen Homeland Security Department
video:video.video:description: The Senate took the first steps in a new effort to reopen the Department of Homeland Security early Thursday, voting to adopt a budget plan that would fund ICE and Border Patrol over Democratic objections and sending it to the House.

video:video.video:title: Building roads can threaten the Amazon. But they may become dig sites that peer into the past
video:video.video:description: Paving roads in the Amazon rainforest often causes deforestation that threatens the people who live there.

video:video.video:title: Volunteers offer free meals during Georgia wildfire evacuations
video:video.video:description: While wildfires are burning through Southeastern Georgia, a community gathered to help evacuees. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: New mega-bridge in Lesotho aims to help nearly double country's water exports to South Africa
video:video.video:description: New mega-bridge in Lesotho aims to help nearly double country's water exports to South Africa

video:video.video:title: Iran’s exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
video:video.video:description: Iran’s exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid as he left a building after a news conference in Berlin.

video:video.video:title: Nicole Wallace says 'House of the Spirits' role came at the right time
video:video.video:description: Nicole Wallace and Alfonso Herrera star in the Prime Video adaptation of Isabel Allende's "The House of the Spirits," a magical epic following generations of the same family in South America.

video:video.video:title: Officials warn of 'high fire activity day' as Georgia wildfires lead to more air quality warnings
video:video.video:description: The two biggest fires in southern Georgia have spread rapidly over the past two days and destroyed more than 50 homes in rural areas. (AP Video: Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
video:video.video:description: Judges at the International Criminal Court on Thursday confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte for deadly anti-drugs crackdowns he allegedly oversaw while in office.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil's brother accuses Israel of targeted assassination as funeral held
video:video.video:description: Thousands of mourners marched on Thursday behind the coffin of the Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil who was killed by Israeli strikes on Wednesday in southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $81 billion takeover of the Hollywood giant
video:video.video:description: An $81 billion Warner-Paramount mega merger has received shareholders’ stamp of approval, propelling a deal that could vastly reshape Hollywood and the wider media landscape closer to the finish line.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 23
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, April 23rd: Trump gives "shoot and kill" order in Strait; Pope Leo urges resumption of talks to end war; Watchdog reviewing DOJ handling of Epstein files; Warner Bros. shareholders approve Paramount takeover

video:video.video:title: Retrial begins in deputy's killing of a Black man entering his grandmother's Ohio home
video:video.video:description: More than two years after a judge declared a mistrial, attorneys began opening statements in a retrial of a former Ohio sheriff’s deputy charged in the December 2020 killing of Casey Goodson Jr.

video:video.video:title: Up close with the wind farms taking shape on US East Coast
video:video.video:description: Offshore wind turbines roughly three times the height of the Statue of Liberty were spinning off the coast of Rhode Island Thursday, sending clean electricity to the region.

video:video.video:title: Family ties in 'The House of the Spirits'
video:video.video:description: Family is a big theme in "The House of the Spirits," a new TV series based on the epic bestseller by Isabel Allende.

video:video.video:title: New Yorkers react to federal order reclassifying medical marijuana
video:video.video:description: While some New Yorkers welcome the move to reclassify state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous drug, recreational dispensaries are still waiting for a full reclassification that would ease their tax burden.

video:video.video:title: Cuba wants dialogue with US, but 'ready to fight back' says ambassador
video:video.video:description: Cuba's U.N. ambassador denied reports that Havana would release political prisoners as part of the renewed dialogue with the U.S. while asserting that officials are “preparing for all scenarios” if President Donald Trump makes good on his threats to intervene in the country.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry meets celebrated Ukrainian medic, receives book by AP photographer in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: On the sidelines of the Kyiv Economic Forum, Prince Harry met with celebrated Ukrainian medic Yuliia Paievska, known as Taira, who documented the siege of Mariupol with a body camera, capturing 256 gigabytes of her team’s desperate efforts over two weeks to save lives.

video:video.video:title: This robot can beat you (and pros) at table tennis
video:video.video:description: This robot isn’t just playing table tennis — it’s sometimes beating human pros, a major leap for machines in the real world.

video:video.video:title: 'My house is gone!' exclaims woman eyeing Georgia wildfire damage
video:video.video:description: A woman recording the damage caused by a wildfire in Brantley County, Georgia cried out, "My house is gone!"

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo urges US and Iran to return to peace talks after creating 'chaotic situation'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV urged the United States and Iran to return to talks to end the war Thursday and condemned capital punishment, in a wide-ranging press conference en route home from his trip to Africa.

video:video.video:title: Grossi, head of IAEA, says UN's effectiveness tied to 20th century charter
video:video.video:description: Rafael Grossi, the director general of the International Atomic Energy Agency and a candidate to be the next secretary-general of the United Nations, told reporters Thursday that the effectiveness of the U.N. is tied to a charter that is 80 years old and not always efficient.

video:video.video:title: Trump reclassifies state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous drug
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's acting attorney general on Thursday signed an order reclassifying state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous drug.

video:video.video:title: Hispanic adults are unhappy with Trump after moving toward him in 2024, AP-NORC polls find
video:video.video:description: A new AP-NORC poll finds Hispanic adults who helped re-elect President Donald Trump in 2024 are now deeply unhappy with his performance. (AP Video: Nathan Ellgren)

video:video.video:title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander shrugs off Dillon Brooks' criticism after Game 2
video:video.video:description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander shrugs off critics after Game 2 win over Suns. He had 37 points and nine assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Phoenix Suns 120-107.

video:video.video:title: Who should be the next James Bond? A few out-of-the-box ideas
video:video.video:description: The bond, James Bond, market is heating up. For almost a year, Denis Villeneuve has been on tap to direct the next 007 installment, the first since Daniel Craig exited the role

video:video.video:title: Meta slashes 10% of its workforce and Microsoft offers buyouts as AI spending soars
video:video.video:description: In the latest round of tech industry job cuts, Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers and Microsoft is offering buyouts to a similar number.

video:video.video:title: Citizenship change has more Americans 'waking up' Canadian
video:video.video:description: A change in the Canada's citizenship laws has prompted a rush of Americans to explore their ancestry and file paperwork seeking dual citizenship.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he won't be rushed to end war as Iran negotiations drag on
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump in an exchange with reporters in the Oval Office pushed back against questions that the conflict is exceeding the four-to-six week timeline that he and aides previously set for the war.

video:video.video:title: Police say 10 hurt, suspects fled after two groups shot at each other inside Mall of Louisiana
video:video.video:description: A shooting between two groups of people inside the food court at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge injured 10 people and at least some of those responsible fled the scene as a huge police response arrived.

video:video.video:title: Nia Long: 'Grace is what creates greatness'
video:video.video:description: Nia Long shares her thoughts on Michael Jackson's mother Katherine Jackson. Long plays the Jackson family matriarch in the biopic "Michael."

video:video.video:title: Georgia officials urge residents near wildfires to remain ready to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A wildfire fueled by shifting winds scorched homes and vehicles in southeastern Georgia Thursday, forcing residents to decide whether to evacuate or stay behind.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Lebanon and Israel agree to extend Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire by 3 weeks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: National Mall installation honors estimated 20,000 children abducted from Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian American organizations have installed a display of 20,000 teddy bears at the National Mall in Washington representing Ukrainian children who have been abducted by Russian forces since the war in Ukraine began.

video:video.video:title: Woman's home destroyed by Georgia wildfires
video:video.video:description: A woman recording the damage caused by a wildfire in Brantley County, Georgia, surveyed the smoky scene of burned out vehicles and other debris.

video:video.video:title: David Bailey is focusing on what he can control ahead of NFL draft
video:video.video:description: David Bailey's focus is on things he can control ahead of tonight's NFL draft. He could potentially be drafted by the New York Jets at No. 2.

video:video.video:title: Francis Mauigoa represents Samoan culture at the NFL Draft
video:video.video:description: Francis Mauigoa is representing Samoan culture at the NFL Draft.

video:video.video:title: Follow AP's 2026 NFL Draft updates
video:video.video:description: The 2026 NFL Draft is tonight, and AP is there. Visit APNews.com for live updates.

video:video.video:title: From beauty to transportation, a lack of water and power forces Cubans to change their routines
video:video.video:description: Severe shortages of water, power, and money, combined with a U.S energy blockade have deepened poverty and increased hunger across Cuba as severe blackouts persist.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian homes in Jerusalem neighborhood demolished 'with impunity' by Israeli authorities
video:video.video:description: Bulldozers removed rubble of homes on Thursday in the al-Bustan neighborhood of Silwan in east Jerusalem, a longstanding flashpoint where Palestinians say Israeli authorities and settlers are working to displace residents.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry praises Ukraine’s resilience and urges stronger support at Kyiv forum
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Prince Harry praised Ukraine’s resilience and urged stronger support for the country at the Kyiv Economic Forum on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Jeremiyah Love wants to represent for running backs
video:video.video:description: Jeremiyah Love wants to represent for running backs. He was drafted by the Arizona Cardinals at No. 3.

video:video.video:title: New threats and attacks in Strait of Hormuz put further strain on Iran-US ceasefire
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday he has ordered the U.S. military to “shoot and kill” small Iranian boats that deploy mines to choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Colombia hosts global summit to tackle fossil fuel reliance
video:video.video:description: Against a backdrop of rising global tensions and energy market instability, governments from around 50 countries will gather Friday in Colombia’s Caribbean city of Santa Marta for a summit aimed at accelerating the shift away from fossil fuels.

video:video.video:title: Carnell Tate on what he brings to an NFL Team
video:video.video:description: Carnell Tate says he can "do it all" when it comes to football. He was drafted by the Tennessee Titans at No. 4.

video:video.video:title: Return to Nature case spurs changes to Colorado funeral industry
video:video.video:description: State officials and industry representatives say reforms adopted by Colorado lawmakers are making a difference after a search found nearly 200 decomposing bodies at the Return to Nature funeral home in 2023.

video:video.video:title: Ethan Hawke on Paramount-Warner Bros merger: 'Life is change'
video:video.video:description: Ethan Hawke is concerned about the Paramount-Warner Bros merger but says "life is change." (April 23)

video:video.video:title: Apollo astronaut Schmitt cheers on new generation of moon missions
video:video.video:description: Apollo astronaut Harrison “Jack” Schmitt knows what the Artemis II crew was feeling when it rocketed into space earlier this month for a historic lunar flyby.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 17-23 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's unaware of US soldier charged with using inside information
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he was unaware of the charges brought to a U.S. soldier involved in the military operation to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro that has been charged with using classified information about the mission to win more than $400,000 in an online betting market, accor

video:video.video:title: Mike Myers says different generations enjoying his work is 'all a dream'
video:video.video:description: Mike Myers says it’s “very, very satisfying” to hear from audiences who are still discovering — and revisiting — his work across generations.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle gives his best Katherine LaNasa 'Baby Jane Doe' impression
video:video.video:description: At the TIME100 Gala, 'The Pitt' star Noah Wyle delivers his best impression of Katherine LaNasa’s viral “Baby Jane Doe” line. (April 23)

video:video.video:title: Man who helped runner who collapsed near Boston Marathon's finish line says he acted on instinct
video:video.video:description: Ajay Haridasse was close to finishing the Boston Marathon when his legs gave out. But as he struggled to get up, two other runners came along, lifted him up and helped him about 1,000 feet (305 meters) to the finish line.

video:video.video:title: Former Chernobyl liquidators return to nuclear site four decades later
video:video.video:description: A group of former cleanup workers who returned to Chernobyl ahead of the 40th anniversary of the 1986 nuclear disaster, revisited a nearby site where they once worked to help contain the world’s worst nuclear accident.

video:video.video:title: Immersive installations, sustainability and latest trends at Milan's design week
video:video.video:description: Milan has once again become the global capital of design as Salone del Mobile and Fuorisalone take over the city.
